text: Average training volume across marathon runners of all levels is approximately 45 km per week in the 16 weeks before a marathon, with high variability.
The fastest marathon runners (2:00-2:30 finish) accumulate more than three times the training volume of slower runners.
population: fast marathon runners (120-150 min finish time)

text: Faster marathon runners achieve higher training volumes primarily by increasing easy (Z1) running, while time spent in Z2 and Z3 remains relatively stable across performance levels.
The proportion of training completed in Z1 shows a strong negative correlation with marathon finish time (faster runners do proportionally more Z1), with R² ≥ 0.90.
Higher proportions of training in Z2 and Z3 are associated with slower marathon times, with R² ≥ 0.85.
A pyramidal training intensity distribution (most volume in Z1, less in Z2, least in Z3) is the most common approach among marathon runners, adopted by over 80% of the fastest runners.
The boundary between Z1 and Z2 for marathon training intensity distribution can be estimated at 82.3% of critical speed.
